angle_convention: Kaggle uses complementary angle: our_angle = 180 - kaggle_angle

analysis.current_bottom_range_coverage: 39.8% of correct squatting frames fall in [70°, 115°]
analysis.label_overlap: All labels have nearly identical knee angle distributions (mean ~86°). Labels distinguish ERROR TYPE, not depth.

knee_valgus_ratio.note: Different scale from our system (our threshold: 0.85)

conclusions: Current thresholds are reasonable for real MediaPipe data
Kaggle dataset is synthetic with different angle convention
Dataset useful for understanding feature distributions, not for direct threshold tuning
Bottom range 70-115° covers ~40% of squatting depth range - appropriate for FSM bottom state
Knee valgus ratio threshold (0.85) is conservative but safe - prevents false positives
recommendations: For better calibration, collect real MediaPipe recordings with manual labels
Consider expanding bottom range to 65-120° for more inclusive depth detection
